candidates.stmt: During my term as an Adur District Councillor I focused on building community groups such as Friends of Old Shoreham and the Adur Estuary SSSI group. The global and national picture is grim. So locally we must foster trust and create friendlier neighbourhoods. I will work with  residents in Buckingham ward to identify the best sources of help, to resolve our shared problems and to create better and more cohesive communities.

This is my first time as a candidate.

I’ve lived in Lancing since 2013. I'm 61 years old, autistic and nonbinary.

I left work as a nurse to care for my disabled wife. We have five kids. As a family, we’re diverse neurologically, and in respect of gender identity and sexual orientation. In my free time I’m writing a novel about a vampire in 1990.

Society is increasingly built around greed, power and planetary harm. We can make a democracy based on fairness.

Looking to make a bold difference both locally and nationally. Like many I am tired of divisive politics, empty promises, and minorities being used as political chess pieces by the big, millionaire backed parties.

Hope, not hate.

I’m standing as your local Green candidate because I believe in a fairer, greener future where everyone feels they belong. I stand for bringing people together and pushing back against division and extremism. We have far more in common than what sets us apart. I’m committed to protecting and expanding access to green spaces, environmental education, and building a stronger, more connected community across Adur, starting with Manor Ward.

Jude has lived in Adur for nearly 50 years, the last 25 in Southwick Green ward. She says: “Bringing up a family and working in education have given me a desire to do what I can to improve facilities and opportunities for children and young people in the ward.

“As co-founder of Kingston Beach Residents’ Association I have for many years been aware of the difficulties we have in providing suitable housing for locals at the same time as maintaining the character of our neighbourhood. I will provide a strong voice to represent existing and new residents while addressing concerns such as excessive traffic, pollution, parking and the loss of green spaces as more construction takes place.

“I know this ward very well as I get around mostly on foot or by bike and this, coupled with a love of swimming, means that I am aware that many areas could be improved. Kingston Beach in particular has become very popular all year round. It now needs to be made more user-friendly with more facilities there. Other parts of the ward could be enhanced by providing recreational areas as well as planting shrubs and trees to offset some of the effects of pollution and encourage wildlife. These greener areas would also support our community’s health and wellbeing.”

Jude’s four children were all educated in local schools from age five to 18.

With your vote, Jude will join Robina Baine as a councillor for Southwick Green.

Troy has been a member of the Green Party since 2022, and recently led the team in developing a localised brand strategy for the Adur area.

Troy has also been active in campaigning for better protection of the Adur Estuary SSSI (Site of Special Scientific Interest).

Troy believes that humans only win if nature wins first, and that everyone deserves to be treated with dignity and respect.

The Rev Pat Alden has been a Labour party member for more than 50 years and attended many Labour party conferences with her late husband, former local councillor and Labour group leader Les Alden.

She says: “I have a background in education and have served as a school governor for over 30 years. I taught for 40 years in London and I am currently vice chair of governors at Sir Robert Woodard Academy, Lancing, where a number of our young people attend from Shoreham.

“I also serve as assistant curate to the United Benefice of St Nicolas Old Shoreham, St Julian and St Giles, Kingston Buci .

“My political agenda is one that takes environmental issues very seriously and sees the impact of climate change on our community.

“I am a supporter of social housing and feel that the council needs to prioritise both social and affordable housing when considering new developments.”

“I am also concerned about the increase in population in our town and the impact it is having on our infrastructure of schools, health and social care. I welcome new development so long as it takes account of these issues and is, of course energy efficient and sustainable.

“Another issue that concerns me is the support of vulnerable families as well as refugees. Our Foodbanks are stretched and the local authority should do all to help in the voluntary sector.”

Lee Cowen, first elected to represent Mash Barn in Lancing in 2017, said: “It’s been an honour to serve the people of Mash Barn over the last four years. Back then the big issue for the ward was the New Monks Farm development and in many ways It still is. I continue to work with the developers and CALA homes to reduce the impact of the development on our community.

“Here are some or my achievements:

Fundraising for the installation of two Automated External Defbrillators     Saving the Lancing Children and Family Centre after it was earmarked for closure by the Conservative-run county council     Ensuring a new dog poo bin was installed on Monks Avenue at the behest of residents     Successfully lobbying the council to change the status of the grazing land behind Manor Close from brownfield to greenfield, reducing the risk of development there     Organising community litter picks     Arranging public consultation for upgrading the Shadwells Road open space     Actioning many issues following the move to fortnight bin collections     Actioning fly-tipping removal as needed     Actioning council housing repairs and lobbied for scrutiny of the service     Actioning CALA Homes to install dust suppression measures     Actioning the removal of privacy invading CCTV installed at the Brighton and Hove Albion academy.”

Lee is leader of the Labour group and leader of the opposition on Adur District Council.

As someone who has spent their career in education, I will use my skills to build a greener, fairer community for our local area. I will champion priorities like improved recycling and affordable housing while protecting our green spaces. I am committed to supporting residents through the cost of living crisis and ensuring our council works for everyone.

I am dedicated and committed to working hard for our community. Improving local services, green spaces and a sense of belonging. 
I listen, act and deliver to the best of my ability. I believe that by working together we can build a fairer future for everyone. I am a volunteer in our local Sompting nature space and park.

I have been a resident of Adur for over 10 years, having moved here from Hampshire where I worked as a science teacher and editor. As a volunteer I support local initiatives such as litter picking, school safer streets and protection of the natural environment. I believe most people want a fairer society and as a councillor I would campaign for improvements in local services such as planning,  traffic management and waste collections.

Dan believes strongly in community and the positive impact that people can have on their local area when they work together. He feels that the Conservative controlled council has let down the residents of Southlands through neglect of council homes, historic lack of investment in Parklands, for example, and the upkeep of Shoreham in general.

As a father of two young children, he knows the benefits that stimulating and well looked after surroundings can have on people’s mental and physical health, as well as their sense of community. He has seen first-hand the impact of poorly maintained council housing on the tenants who live there and the unnecessary distress caused by persistent under-funding.

Dan has worked as a teacher for 13 years both in Sussex and in east London.

He says: “I believe strongly in the value of public service. I believe the needs of the most vulnerable of our young people must be met and they must be provided with the tools they need to succeed in life. That means well resourced services. With many people now feeling the impact of price rises and tighter household budgets, I will  make sure that the council works hard to provide residents with the services they need.”

I am a life-long Adur resident as well as a parent and heritage worker.

I am passionate about my environment and to preserving the things which make our area special, including the sea, the River Adur and the Downs. I am also committed to ensuring effective services which meet the needs of all residents, as well as to planning for a more equitable and sustainable future.

I have been a St Mary's councillor since 2021 and have responded to and taken action where possible on issues raised by residents.
As leader of the council since 2024 I have sought to turn the council around after years of failing to deliver on important projects for local people. We are taking pride in Adur and getting things done, such as finally finishing the Shoreham Flood Wall, which will be completed this year.
We’re supporting people with the cost of living, helping residents secure pension credit, reduced water bills, fuel vouchers and energy grants.
We’re delivering and improving homes, including urgently needed council homes
And we’re making Adur greener: planting over four thousand trees, no longer using diesel for our bin lorries, stopping the use of bee-harming herbicides, improving  our parks and playgrounds and we will be  iaunching a weekly food bin collection scheme that will see residents’ food waste recycled into fertiliser and energy. 
See more about Labour's leadership of the council here: https://www.ewslabour.og.uk

Local Election, St Mary's Ward

I am standing for election in St Mary's Ward to bring the Green Party's message of hope and unity to Adur District Council.
I believe that Adur District Council needs more Green voices influencing decsions that affect local people. There should be  collaboration between the people who are elected to represent our area based on the needs of all residents rather than serving internal party politics. Greens are free to speak up and represent the views of local people; they are not told how to vote by the national party
I will work hard for Shoreham to become a greener, kinder, fairer place for all who live and work here. Hope can become normal again.

West Sussex County Council Elections, Shoreham North

I am standing for the Shoreham North Division for West Sussex County Council as I believe that having more Green county councillors will lead to better representation of everyday people.  Greens are free to speak up for residents and are not told how to vote by the national party.  I will work hard all year round,  not just at election time, to find out what matters to local people and ensure that concerns about county council matters, such as the state of the roads, transport, social care and education are heard and dealt with.  The two party political system is broken and it is time to have Greens making hope normal again in local government.

I am standing for Adur District Council to deliver practical, local solutions. I want to protect our environment and river, which brought me so much joy as a child. I believe in honesty, compassion, and listening first to residents’ concerns. Together, we can keep our communities safe, clean, and thriving while planning responsibly for future generations. I will work hard to represent everyone fairly, openly, and with real dedication to our area.

I grew up in Adur and want to see decisions made here by normal people like myself, and make positive change in my local community. I have a particular interest in Transport, Equality and Culture. If elected I promise to be hard-working, easily accessible and be a voice for regular people who work and live in Adur.

A Labour party member since the early 1980s, Joe O’Halloran is a writer/consultant with over 30 years of experience as a journalist in the IT, broadcasting, CGI, telecoms, design sectors and as a consultant in marketing as well as a safety steward at Brighton & Hove Albion.

He has been a resident of Shoreham-by-Sea since 2007. Politically, Joe is driven by a desire to strive for equality and fairness in our community.

Equality of opportunity and fairness in the delivery of services, especially to those who need them most; equality and fairness in access to the advanced IT and communications services that our working and home lives increasingly depend on; equality and fairness in people’s ability to obtain essential utilities such as electricity and gas; equality and fairness in the terms of affordable housing. As councillor, Joe would strive to put these issues foremost and serve better the people of Shoreham.
